channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Release Date Leaked
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming Star Wars racing game, Star Wars: Galactic Racer, has new details confirmed on its official Steam page.

The game, which was revealed at the 2025 Game Awards, is officially coming on October 6, 2026, according to a new graphic showcasing the pre-order bonuses. Those who pre-order the title will receive a bonus Livery for their Repulsorcraft and an exclusive player Banner that can be used in the multiplayer modes.

That’s just for those who pre-order the standard edition of the game. Depending on how much money you’re willing to spend, there are even more goodies.

Star Wars: Galactic Racer Deluxe Edition Description

The Steam page has also revealed what’s in store for those who pre-order the game’s deluxe edition. In addition to the pre-order bonuses mentioned above, there are also exclusive Vehicles, a digital art book, three exclusive arcade events, and more.

Check out the full write-up below:

​​​The Star Wars: Galactic Racer™ Deluxe Edition features: 

  • Three exclusive vehicles: Become the envy of the paddock and pilot the Kor Sarun: Ciza T speeder bike, the Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder, and the Kor Sarun: Rak S skim speeder 
  • Unique Livery for pilots who wish to race in style. Coat any of your speeders with an exclusive Livery inspired by the Naboo N-1 starfighter 
  • Three Arcade Events for the fearless racers. The Galactic League challenges you to three exclusive Arcade trials designed specifically for your Kor Sarun repulsorcraft 
  • Deluxe Player Banner containing an exclusive Insignia and Background for multiplayer modes 
  • Digital Art Book showcasing never-before-seen character, vehicle, and location artwork from the development of Star Wars: Galactic Racer™ 

As of yet, there are no official price points for either edition of the game. It was recently revealed that the game will launch with Nvidia DLSS 4.5. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Are you looking forward to playing Star Wars: Galactic Racer? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Call of Duty Dev Has a Lead Role on Tarkov Head’s New Game
item.content:encoded:
Rant Gaming Studios, the developer behind Fragmentary Order, has revealed that Aaron Beck, former Call of Duty concept artist, is leading the conceptual work on the all-new shooter. Fragmentary Order is another ideation from the mind of Nikita Buyanov, the face of Escape from Tarkov, the ten-year-old extraction shooter that defined the entire genre.

In a post on social media, it was explained that Beck has worked with Rant Gaming on Fragmentary Order for more than a year and a half. Fans of Beck’s work have already spotted some of his trademark stylisation in Frago’s earliest content.

Rant Gaming Studios is ‘Cooking Hard’ on Frago

Fragmentary Order was only revealed a few weeks ago, but it has already grabbed the extraction market by the shoulders and given it a shake. That’s interesting, because it’s not an extraction title. But, with the clearest of ties to Nikita Buyanov and everything he has created over the years, it’s easy to imagine Escape from Tarkov’s fanbase crossing over into Fragmentary Order.

In a post on social media, Buyanov revealed that Aaron Beck, a former Call of Duty concept artist, has been fleshing out Frago’s style for many months already:

Beck is best known for leading the conceptual creation of assets in games like Call of Duty: Infinite Warfare, Modern Warfare, and Modern Warfare II. He also contributed to Black Ops III and Warzone’s 2.0 shift in 2022.

Once upon a time, Beck worked with Weta Workshop, a prestigious production studio, and worked on the likes of Avatar, Elysium, and Mad Max.

Even the quickest glance through Beck’s portfolio reveals a passion for mechanical creations, robotics, mechanized suits, and exoskeletons, almost all of which are front and centre in Fragmentary Order.

In a follow-up post, the Fragmentary Order team wrote:

Aaron is our Lead Concept Artist and cooking hard with this one, we are planning to share some in game concepts in nearest future.

Fans were quick to notice that in the CORIE ARG that was used to tease Fragmentary Order, a soldier called Sergeant Beck was mentioned in some of the game’s documents and lore. They’ve theorized that this is a nod to the artist.

Are you happy to hear that an artist with serious chops is aligned with Fragmentary Order? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a Battlefield movie is being developed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
